heading: Multiyear contracts: purchase of electricity from renewable energy sources
label: 2922i Multiyear contracts: purchase of electricity from renewable energy sources
notes: Amendments2021— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283section 2410q of this title

Effective Date of 2021 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 116–283section 1801(d) of Pub. L. 116–283section 3001 of this titleJan. 1, 2022
amendments: Amendments2021— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283section 2410q of this title

Effective Date of 2021 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 116–283section 1801(d) of Pub. L. 116–283section 3001 of this titleJan. 1, 2022

children.heading: .—Multiyear Contracts Authorized
children.label: a .—Multiyear Contracts Authorized
children.text: Subject to subsection (b), the Secretary of Defense may enter into a contract for a period not to exceed 10 years for the purchase of electricity from sources of renewable energy, as that term is defined in section 203(b)(2) of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 ().42 U.S.C. 15852(b)(2)

children.heading: .—Limitations on Contracts for Periods in Excess of Five Years
children.label: b .—Limitations on Contracts for Periods in Excess of Five Years

children.text: the proposed purchase of electricity under such contract is cost effective for the Department of Defense; and
it would not be possible to purchase electricity from the source in an economical manner without the use of a contract for a period in excess of five years.

children.heading: .—Relationship to Other Multiyear Contracting Authority
children.label: c .—Relationship to Other Multiyear Contracting Authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to preclude the Department of Defense from using other multiyear contracting authority of the Department to purchase renewable energy.
